steps.title: Substitute the known quantity into the equation
steps.content:
Since there are 9 blue iris flowers, substitute $x = 9$ into the equation:
$$3(9) + 4y = 67$$

steps.title: Solve for the number of white iris flowers
steps.content:
Simplify and isolate $y$:
$$27 + 4y = 67$$
$$4y = 40$$
$$y = 10$$
Verify: $3(9) + 4(10) = 27 + 40 = 67$ ✓

There are 10 white iris flowers. That matches Choice D — 10.
